content.problemStatement:
Rectify the errors in the program to get the desired output.

Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 10). Each of the next T lines contains a single integer choice.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line. If choice is 1, 2 or 3 print "Option X selected" where X is the choice. For any other value print "Invalid choice".

solution.approach: The task is a direct mapping from the input integer to a fixed output string. For each test case we read the integer and use a switch (or if‑else) to decide which message to print. Choices 1, 2 and 3 have dedicated messages, all other values fall into the default case which prints "Invalid choice". The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
